commit.message:
v2.1.0: Fix slot collision, dispose race, CQ overflow recovery

Slot collision (High):
- Add generation counter to UserData (16-bit, per-slot). CQEs from
  previous connections are detected and silently discarded.

DisposeAsync race (High):
- Add volatile _disposing flag. CompleteSend checks it before accessing
  shared state, preventing double-dispose with Kestrel's thread.

CQ overflow recovery (Critical):
- On overflow detection, scan all connections with stale HasSendInFlight/
  HasRecvInFlight. Force-dispose handles, close sockets, signal send
  loops to break. Recovers gracefully instead of leaking resources.

Also:
- UnsafeInlineScheduling option (default true, set false for safety)
- Partial send retry loop (handles short kernel writes)
- MemoryHandle leak fix (dispose on any failure between Pin and SQE)
- Infinite error loop fix (force-advance CQ after 5 consecutive errors)
- Remove dead code (PendingSend, OnSendComplete, PooledSendCompletion)

Lab benchmark: 2,559,694 RPS @ 1024c TC=28 (no regression from v2.0)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(player): speed lock popup, youtube-style seek, double-badge fix

- Add speed lock popup: tap 2x badge → fullscreen overlay with slider
  and lock toggle. When locked, video plays at chosen speed without
  holding (range 0.25x–5x).
- Block hold gesture when speed is locked. Fix stale-closure bug by
  re-keying pointerInput on isSpeedLocked/lockedSpeed so the handler
  always captures fresh values.
- YouTube-style accumulated seek: each double-tap adds ±10 s to signed
  accumulator; seek fires 600 ms after the last tap. Tapping the
  opposite side reduces the accumulated amount.
- SeekAnimOverlay: AnimatedContent slide-up animation on seek number
  change; no zero-flicker on fade-out.
- Bump versionCode 1 → 2, versionName 1.0.0 → 1.1.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Turn enemies into animated slimes with jitter particle attack

- Created slime spritesheet (assets/slime_spritesheet.png) with idle,
  move-left, move-right, and jitter animation rows
- Added SlimeAttackComponent: handles infrequent jitter+particle attacks
  that damage the player on contact
- Added AnimationComponent to enemies with slime animations driven by
  SlimeAttackComponent based on movement/attack state
- Modified EnemyAIComponent with pause support so enemies stop patrolling
  during jitter attack phase
- Enemies are now green animated blobs with the same patrol behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: split keyed/named access detection + remove dead object creation code

- Split has_keyed_access_sites into separate has_keyed_access_sites
  (for array IC allocation) and has_named_property_sites (for R15
  TLS caching). Previously, LoadNamedGeneric was incorrectly included
  in has_keyed_access_sites, causing array IC slots to be allocated
  for functions that only do named property access (like deep_object).

- Remove references to unimplemented clone_object_literal_template_with_values
  and promote_object_literal_template_with_values methods in
  create_object_with_props_inner that were causing CI compile failures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: remove sensitive infra details from README

- Remove temporary Cloud Run URL (direct endpoint exposure)
- Remove individual Secret Manager secret names (attack surface map)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(email): sync daily quotas from provider APIs before sending

Replace in-memory-only counters with real usage data from each
provider's API at the start of every cascade run:
- Mailgun: GET /v3/{domain}/stats/total?event=accepted&duration=1d
- Mailjet: GET /v3/REST/statcounters (daily resolution)
- Unosend: GET /v1/emails (filter by today's date)
- Resend: GET /emails (filter by today's date)

This ensures accurate quota tracking across separate workflow runs
instead of relying on 429 responses to detect exhausted limits.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Round 3 codebase cleanup — security fixes, endpoint dedup, frontend types

Phase 1: Security — redact redis_url from /cache/config, remove internal cache job endpoints, fix missing await in batch cancel
Phase 2: Remove duplicate batch endpoints from processing_routes (~45 lines)
Phase 3: Consolidate graph search — remove /search/entities, /search/frames, /search/advanced; enhance /search/hybrid with video_ids, entity_types, graph expansion toggle
Phase 4: Standardize all 17+ dual video_id OR patterns to single v.video_id across 6 files
Phase 5: Frontend type dedup — replace 7 duplicate Scene/Chapter/TranscriptSegment definitions with imports from @/types; remove debug console.logs; add null-safety for scene.duration
Phase 6: Frontend component audit — confirmed 'use client' removal has zero bundle benefit; deferred
Phase 7: Remove 5 dead endpoints (ffmpeg, ffmpeg/batch, 2x /search, jobs/submit); clean orphaned imports and dead test functions

30 files changed, +152/-1089 lines, 1184 tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: make ensureDefaultAssets non-fatal with try/catch and @ suppression

mkdir permission errors are non-fatal cosmetic assets, should not abort install.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix release workflow verification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix ReboxLocal missing from bytecode merge/adjust and eval for-loop local visibility

Three locations scanned eval/module bytecode to skip operands but did not
account for ReboxLocal (opcode 109, 1-byte operand):

1. merge_eval_chunk in vm.rs — bytecode copier that adjusts constant
   indices and jump targets when merging eval code into the host chunk.
2. Non-configurable property check scanner in vm.rs — pre-scan of eval
   bytecode to detect illegal redefinitions.
3. adjust_merged_bytecode in opcode.rs — module bytecode IP/constant
   offset adjuster.

Missing ReboxLocal caused its 1-byte operand to be interpreted as the
next opcode, shifting all subsequent bytes and corrupting the entire
code stream. This manifested as panics (index out of bounds, unhandled
opcode 109) when eval or module code contained for-let loops.

Also extended top_level_locals_at_ip snapshots to cover all scope depths
at function_depth==0 (not just scope_depth==0), and added snapshot
recording at for-loop init entry/exit. This allows eval inside a
top-level for(let ...) body to see the loop variable.

Fixes: tagged-template/cache-eval-inner-function (cluster 15)
Fixes: module-code/namespace/define-own-property (clusters 10, 18)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: O(N) loadAllPackets in /kit-by-slug, path traversal guard, remove stale test file

- Replace loadAllPackets() scan with direct single-file read in /kit-by-slug
  endpoint: reads ARTIFACTS_DIR/{slug}/figma/reconstruction.json directly,
  avoiding O(N) disk reads and JSON parsing for all kits on every request
- Add slug allowlist validation (/^[a-z0-9-]+$/i) and path.sep traversal
  guard before path.join to prevent directory traversal attacks
- Decode with try/catch to handle malformed percent-encoded slugs gracefully
- Remove services/presentationState.test.ts (already moved to
  src/test/services/presentationState.test.ts in previous commit)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Introduce a complete Go gRPC client/server implementation for a
ping/pong service, along with tooling and CI infrastructure.

  Go bindings into pb/ using protoc
- Implement the gRPC server (pong) and client (ping) as Cobra
  subcommands in cmd/

  final stage produces a minimal alpine image
- Add a Makefile with targets for proto generation, build, lint
  (go vet + optional golangci-lint), test, and clean
- Add a GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/docker-publish.yml)
  that builds and publishes the container image to ghcr.io on pushes
  to main and version tags, with GHA layer caching
- Add .gitignore to exclude the compiled binary and OS artifacts

commit.message:
EXP-1191-1200: Grand Final benchmark — production R²=0.630 (5-fold CV)

Key results from 200-experiment campaign finale:
- EXP-1191: Combined+AR full stack R²=0.676 (single split, 11/11)
- EXP-1192: AR(3) optimal, nonlinear AR catastrophically overfits
- EXP-1193: Multi-horizon: 30min=0.785, 60min=0.535, 120min=0.220
- EXP-1194: Recursive prediction fails (-0.153, error accumulation)
- EXP-1197: Online learning +0.016 (10/11 patients)
- EXP-1200: Grand Final 5-fold CV: offline=0.488, production=0.630

Production pipeline: XGBoost + enhanced features + AR(2) correction
AR coefficients: α≈0.60 (lag-1), β≈-0.29 (lag-2) — damped oscillation
Gap to noise ceiling: 0.224 R² (CGM noise accounts for 0.146)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(excel): add unit tests for XlsxTables cell address utilities (XT001-XT008) (#185)

Add XlsxTablesUtilTests covering SplitAddress, IndexToColumnAddress, and
ColumnAddressToIndex — three pure utility functions used throughout the
spreadsheet data-access layer with no prior dedicated unit tests.

Tests include:
- XT001: SplitAddress splits valid addresses into column/row parts
- XT002: SplitAddress throws FileFormatException when no row number
- XT003: IndexToColumnAddress returns correct letters for known indices (A, Z, AA, ZZ, AAA, ZZZ)
- XT004: IndexToColumnAddress throws for out-of-range index (≥ 18278)
- XT005: ColumnAddressToIndex returns correct 0-based index for known addresses
- XT006: ColumnAddressToIndex throws FileFormatException for 4+ char input
- XT007: Round-trip IndexToColumnAddress → ColumnAddressToIndex is identity
- XT008: Round-trip ColumnAddressToIndex → IndexToColumnAddress is identity

49 tests pass. dotnet csharpier check passes.

commit.message:
chore: update AWF_VERSION to 0.25.16 (#139)

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): true-inline array store + IC-preserving fast path

Zero-call inline fast path for keyed stores of Smi and Boolean values:
on IC hit (same array, in bounds), write discriminant + payload bytes
directly to the element memory without any Rust function call.

Critically, in-bounds stores no longer invalidate the array IC slot.
Previously every StaKeyedProperty zeroed the IC handle, forcing the
next LdaKeyedProperty through the slow path.  Now only out-of-bounds
stores (which may cause Vec reallocation) invalidate the cache.

Added mov_store_dword_base_disp32 and mov_store_byte_imm_base_disp32
instructions to the x86-64 macro assembler for field-level writes.

Expected impact: sieve_primes benchmark (tight load/store loop on
boolean arrays) should improve dramatically since load IC now stays
valid across iterations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: migrate 5 crawlers to central location config (Files 6-10)

Replace hardcoded canton/location values with getCompanyDefaults() calls:
- update-caseificio-gottardo-jobs.mjs (4 replacements)
- update-casale-jobs.mjs (4 replacements in job object)
- update-centiel-jobs.mjs (3 replacements)
- update-citta-di-bellinzona-jobs.mjs (3 replacements)
- update-citta-di-locarno-jobs.mjs (3 replacements)

All files now use HQ.canton, HQ.addressRegion, HQ.postalCode from
the central crawler-location-config.mjs registry.

commit.message:
improve LLM prompt: extract takeaways, not paraphrases

- Stricter system prompt: max 7-10 concise bullets, ~15 words each
- Focus on decisions, action items, facts — ignore filler
- Merge related ideas, update in place, no duplicates
- Lower temperature (0.2) and max_tokens (512) for focus

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: strengthen CI, add branch protections scaffold, templates, Dependabot

- Split CI into 4 parallel jobs: typecheck, lint, build+package, security audit
- Add release workflow triggered on v* tags (builds .vsix, creates GitHub Release)
- Add CodeQL workflow (on push/PR + weekly schedule)
- Add Dependabot config (npm + GitHub Actions, weekly, grouped)
- Add PR template with checklist
- Add bug report and feature request issue templates
- Add CODEOWNERS pointing all review requests to @Lash12
- Add typecheck npm script (tsc --noEmit)
- Configure repo: squash-only merges, delete branch on merge
- Rename default branch master → main

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Release v0.0.10: add state pipeline debug logging

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add targeted debug logging for blind state pipeline

- api/client.py: Log full raw websocket frame and parsed values for BlindPosition events
  - DeviceGuid, DataType, state.Channel, state.Position, state.RawPosition
- coordinator.py: Log state callback invocation and shutter lookup/update
  - Callback entry, DeviceGuid, Channel, Position, TaskExecution
  - Unique ID lookup and shutter object lookup results
  - Shutter state before/after update
- cover.py: Log property access
  - current_cover_position, is_closed, is_opening, is_closing
  - All log values being read from shutter object

All logs use [STATE-RAW], [STATE-COORD], or [STATE-COVER] prefixes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: restore maccatalyst prefix for build ApplicationId (#555)

Build needs maccatalyst.nl.versluis.polypilot to match the provisioning
profile. Upload already uses xcrun altool --upload-package with explicit
Apple ID to bypass the bundle ID lookup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use redirect()->away() in CheckInstalled to avoid localhost redirect

APP_URL may be 'localhost' (Docker env override) before install completes.
redirect()->to() respects APP_URL config, generating wrong localhost URLs.
Switch to redirect()->away() with explicit request host, matching the same
fix already applied in InstallController.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add Cloudflare Workers AI + Mistral AI providers, expand existing models

- Add 2 new providers: Cloudflare Workers AI (cf/) and Mistral AI (mistral/)
- Add 10 Cloudflare models (Llama 3.3 70B, Llama 4 Scout, QwQ 32B, etc.)
- Add 3 Mistral AI models (Small, Codestral, Ministral 8B)
- Add 8 new GitHub Models (gpt-5, o1, o3, Grok-3, Grok-3-Mini, etc.)
- Add 4 new Cohere models (reasoning, translate, Aya 32B, R7B)
- Add 2 new Groq models (compound-mini, Kimi K2 0905)
- Add Cerebras gpt-oss-120b
- Update DEFAULT_CHAIN with 29 new models across all providers
- Update MAX_COMPLETION_TOKENS_MODELS for new reasoning models
- Update MODEL_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS for new Cohere models
- Add CF_API_TOKEN, CF_ACCOUNT_ID, MISTRAL_API_KEY to RC env + workflows
- Bump to v14: 100+ models across 13 providers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(capture): publish dashboard snapshots atomically

Avoid serving partially written BMP files by publishing versioned
snapshots through a manifest that current and previous aliases
resolve through.

This keeps change detection and image-region extraction aligned
with the latest published image while pruning stale snapshots
over time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
